title: annona stenophylla subsp. cuneata
intro: Annona stenophylla subsp. cuneata, also known as the bush pear, is a species of flowering plant in the custard apple family, Annonaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its rich content of annonaceous acetogenins. The health benefits of Annona stenophylla subsp. cuneata include its ability to help manage diabetes, reduce the risk of certain types of cancer, and exhibit neuroprotective effects. Annona stenophylla subsp. cuneata can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, capsules, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
